payload.text:
**Envisioning the MVP**

I'm now focusing on a modular dashboard MVP, prioritizing card-based widgets. Drag-and-drop functionality and real-time updates are critical features. I'm exploring integration possibilities with ClickUp, YouTube/KPI, Google Drive, Suno, and ElevenLabs. "Musikkproduksjon" and "Pros" are also relevant.


**Defining Widget Components**

I'm now concretizing the widget structure, with components for ClickUp, Creative projects, Mental Health, KPIs/Finance, and more. API integrations are in my crosshairs. I've chosen Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind, Zustand, and a React-Grid-Layout mimic for the grid. The visual spec calls for a dark background, neon/pastel accents, pulsing progress bars, and Kanban-style cards.


**Prioritizing Core Features**

I'm now focusing on a lean set of core widgets: Music (project progress), ClickUp (task management), Creative (status and inspiration), Health (daily check-in), and KPIs (YouTube/Streaming data). I'm solidifying the data structures for each. API integrations for Gemini, Suno, and ElevenLabs are coming next. I'm prioritizing performance while using Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind, and a custom grid layout. The visual design will be dark with neon accents and pulsing progress bars.


**Constructing Widget Architecture**

\n\nI'm now concentrating on the architectural breakdown, separating the main layout logic into `App.tsx` while each widget resides in its own component file. I'm focusing on building components for Music (with progress bars representing recording, mix, and mastering phases), Health (a check-in form for mood, energy, and focus), and the foundational structure for other widgets.  I'm also preparing for API integrations with Gemini, considering real-time simulation using `useEffect` intervals to mimic live updates as per the specifications.


**Developing Widget Data Flow**

I'm currently concentrating on the implementation of a sidebar for skills.  I'm also mapping out the central data flow. I'll include components for YouTube analytics using Recharts for CTR and retention.  I'm also working on the Creative module, incorporating a material list and an AI-driven inspiration feed. I've designed an integrations component that visually indicates connection status.  I've decided to use Gemini models for all my text generation. I'm focusing on "Store" implementation. I'll make the widgets draggable or sortable. I'm also ensuring the dashboard uses Google Fonts for visual appeal.

entries.description: App metadata and configuration for the Skaldveg Dashboard.

React application entry point.

Service for interacting with Gemini API for next-step suggestions and inspiration.
Reusable Widget wrapper component with expand/collapse logic.
Music Production widget module.
ClickUp Task management widget module.
Mental Health and personal development widget module.
KPI and Economy widget using Recharts for visualization.
API Integration status widget module.
Main application component with layout and global state management.

payload.text: In the IntegrationWidget, when an integration has a 'WARN' or 'ISSUE' status, add a small info icon that expands to show a brief explanation or last error message.

**Visualizing Integration Flows**

I'm currently focused on the visual aspect of the `IntegrationWidget`. I'm trying to translate the dark, tech-inspired design elements and the "JACKDASH" logo into the component's UI. My objective is to create a sleek, modern interface that clearly reflects the integrations it manages. I'm experimenting with different layouts and color palettes to achieve the right balance between functionality and visual appeal.


**Integrating New Features**

I've shifted gears to integrating Gemini AI features into the IntegrationWidget. The goal is to incorporate conversational voice functionality, image analysis, text-to-speech, audio transcription, and video understanding. I'm focusing on adding informative icons for "WARN" and "ISSUE" statuses that expand with details. The challenge is balancing the new features with the existing UI, all while maintaining the "JACKDASH" aesthetic, which includes a dark textured background, specific colors, and glowing accents.


**Refining UI Elements**

I'm currently working on updating the visual elements of the IntegrationWidget. I've updated the color scheme to match the provided palette, swapping from "SKALDVEG" to "JACKDASH" and incorporating the "OPERATIV KONTROLL I MØRKET" tagline. Icons are being updated to reflect the image's "SYMBOLS" section, and I'm implementing the "Compact Card" and "Expanded Card" styles. Moreover, I'm working on adding the new info icons for "WARN" and "ISSUE" statuses.


**Implementing AI Features**

I'm now prioritizing the integration of AI features into the widget. My plan is to start with a "Video Analysis" skill, as well as the addition of informative icons that will expand to show specific details for "WARN" and "ISSUE" statuses. I'm also revising the code to accommodate error messages for integrations and more detailed project information. The background texture, font changes, and glassmorphism styling are now on my radar to apply these new updates.


**Incorporating New AI Capabilities**

I'm now integrating the requested AI features, specifically focusing on Live Voice Interaction, image analysis, audio transcription, text-to-speech, and video analysis. To keep the UI organized, I'm considering a "Command Center" or "AI Toolkit" section. I'll maintain the Jackdash theme with its dark background, hexagon logo, and cyan/lavender color scheme. I'm prioritizing the `IntegrationWidget`, but I need to incorporate those new abilities in a new design specification.


**Developing New Widgets**

I'm now focusing on three new widgets. I'm adding a "Waveform" visual and "Speak Summary" capability to the `MusicWidget`. I'm creating a new `LiveAudioWidget` for conversational voice and a `VisionWidget` for image and video analysis. I've begun writing the code for these, incorporating the visual elements and functionalities for each.

entries.description: Updated styling to match JACKDASH aesthetic: textured dark background, custom font weights, and neon accents.
Updated types to include integration error details and project metadata.
Updated IntegrationWidget with expandable error messages and info icons for WARN/ISSUE states.
New Vision analysis widget for image and video understanding using Gemini Pro.
Live Conversational AI widget using Gemini Native Audio (Live API).
Updated App to reflect JACKDASH branding and integrated new widgets (Vision, Live Audio).
Enhanced MusicWidget with waveform visuals and a 'Speak Summary' (TTS) feature using Gemini TTS.

payload.text: In `services/geminiService.ts`, add error handling for the AI model calls to gracefully manage API errors and provide informative messages to the user.

**Addressing Gemini API Errors**

I'm currently working on enhancing error handling for the Gemini API calls. I'm focusing on incorporating `try...catch` blocks around the `generateContent` invocations within `services/geminiService.ts`. The aim is to specifically manage 4xx/5xx errors, perhaps by logging the specific HTTP status code or retrying the call with a brief delay. I want to create robust, graceful failure pathways.


**Refining Error Handling Strategies**

I've been drilling down on the specific error types I need to handle in `geminiService.ts`. Alongside catching 4xx and 5xx errors, I'm now also considering network issues, which may manifest as timeouts or connection problems. Furthermore, I'm giving thought to how to gracefully return informative fallback messages when the API is unavailable or returns an unexpected response. I'm keen to ensure that the application remains functional even when the AI component falters.


**Improving API Call Robustness**

I'm expanding the error handling in `geminiService.ts` to cover the new functions. I'm adding `try...catch` blocks around calls to `generateNextStep`, `generateCreativeInspiration`, and `generateSunoPrompt`. To enhance maintainability, I'm going to extract the error management into a separate helper function to avoid duplicated code. The goal is to gracefully manage various API failure scenarios, which includes logging errors and returning suitable fallback values.


**Optimizing Error Responses**

I'm now refining the error handling in `geminiService.ts` to provide user-friendly fallback messages. I've analyzed where the `generate...` functions are used and realized that descriptive error strings are the best approach.  I'm focusing on ensuring these messages are clear and helpful, considering the context of each function's usage.


**Crafting Specific Error Messages**

I've been working on custom error messages within `geminiService.ts` for different failure scenarios. I've implemented logic to identify and handle specific errors, providing informative feedback to the user. For instance, I'm now checking for invalid API keys, rate limit issues (returning a "retrying shortly" message), and server errors (returning a generic "server error"). Additionally, any other errors are categorized as "operational anomalies." This refined approach should significantly improve the user experience.


**Extending Robustness to New Functions**

I'm now integrating error handling into the new `generateSunoPrompt` and related `getAI` function calls. I've embedded `try...catch` blocks to capture potential exceptions. Specifically, the fallback message in `generateSunoPrompt` has been updated to return a default Suno prompt when there's an issue with the API response, maintaining a degree of function. After reviewing `MusicWidget.tsx`, I realized I don't need to add additional error handling there.

**Refining Fallback Mechanisms**

I'm now focusing on integrating informative error messages directly within `generateSunoPrompt` in `services/geminiService.ts`, ensuring clearer feedback to the user on potential API issues. This refinement includes a specific fallback within `generateSunoPrompt` in addition to what's done in `MusicWidget.tsx`. I'm going to return specific error strings from the service so that calling components can display it. This will greatly improve user experience by explaining what went wrong when the AI call fails.


**Enhancing API Call Responses**

I'm now focusing on implementing the specific changes within `services/geminiService.ts`. I'm integrating enhanced error handling within the existing code structure, specifically the `handleAIError` function. I will address various error scenarios using specific conditions. For example, I'm going to return specific error messages for rate limit errors, API key issues, and generic server errors. I'll test the updated error handling logic thoroughly to ensure it functions as intended.


**Developing Fallback Error Texts**

I'm now focusing on improving the fallback error texts used in `generateNextStep`. I've added specific error messages for various failure scenarios. For example, I have incorporated an intelligent message for scenarios where API calls are throttled, server-side issues and content filter blocks. I have made the responses concise and informative, ensuring a better user experience even when errors occur.


**Improving Fallback Prompts**

I'm now integrating error handling into the new `generateCreativeInspiration` function.  I've embedded `try...catch` blocks to capture exceptions.  The current approach has a general fallback. I'll focus on providing detailed, informative error messages for different failure scenarios within this function, much like I did with `generateSunoPrompt`. This change should improve how users receive information about potential API issues, leading to a much smoother user experience.


**Simplifying Retry Logic**

I'm now revising my error handling approach. Although retry logic was initially considered, I've decided to prioritize clear error messages for now, responding more directly to the requirements. I'm focusing on ensuring informative fallback messages for various API error scenarios.


**Enhancing User Feedback**

I'm now revising my error handling approach. I'm focusing on ensuring informative fallback messages for various API error scenarios. I'm prioritizing providing clear error messages, now, over implementing complex retry logic. This approach responds more directly to user requirements.


**Optimizing Error Message Clarity**

I'm now integrating error message clarity into the updated error handling functions. I've focused on creating more informative error messages for different failure modes. Specifically, I've developed more user-friendly messages for key issues, rate limiting, and server errors. I've also added a safety filter trigger response. I believe this improved approach will enhance the user experience.


**Improving Error Clarity**

I'm now expanding error handling within `generateSunoPrompt`. I've added a fallback that returns a generic Suno prompt if API issues arise, ensuring function continuation. Specific error strings will be returned to the calling component to give users better insights into what's failing. This ensures a more informative approach.

**Validating Error Handling**

I'm now meticulously reviewing my changes to `geminiService.ts` for final validation. I've rechecked to make sure error handling is robust, with `try...catch` blocks encompassing all relevant API calls. I've confirmed that the code gracefully manages errors and provides informative messages, as per requirements. I'm satisfied that my updates effectively address the stated error handling objectives.

entries.description: Implemented robust error handling for Gemini API calls with informative, context-aware error messages in the JACKDASH aesthetic.

payload.text: In the LiveAudioWidget, implement a history of past audio sessions, including duration and status.

**Reviewing Session History**

I'm currently fleshing out the design for incorporating a session history feature into the `LiveAudioWidget`. My plan now includes storing the session duration, status ("Completed," "Failed," etc.), and the implied timestamp for each entry. I'm focusing on the data structure and how to efficiently display this information to the user in the widget.


**Defining Session History Implementation**

I'm now fully immersed in the implementation details for the session history within the `LiveAudioWidget`. I've defined the `SessionHistory` type, added the `history` state using `useState`, and integrated a `startTime` ref to capture session start times. The `startSession` and `stopSession` functions are being updated to appropriately record start times, calculate session durations, and append new entries to the history state. Currently focusing on the optimal rendering method for the history list, considering constraints on space and the goal to display the most recent 3-5 sessions.


**Integrating History Display**

I'm now thinking about how to render the session history within the `LiveAudioWidget`. My current idea is to include a "Recent Logs" list directly before the action buttons. This will show the session duration and status for each entry. I'll make sure it uses the JACKDASH aesthetic with a mono font, cyan/slate colors, and small text. I've considered alternatives like a dedicated "History" section but this seems most intuitive.


**Finalizing Session Log Display**

I've decided to refine the session log display. Each entry will show the session's formatted duration (MM:SS) and status ("OK" or "ERR"). Time to start implementing this in the code, ensuring the display matches the overall JACKDASH style.

entries.description: Implemented session history tracking in LiveAudioWidget, including duration calculation, status logging, and a scrollable UI list for past sessions.

payload.text: In the IntegrationWidget, refine the display of error messages. When an error message is present, ensure the info icon is clearly visible and the expanded error message has a distinct visual style (e.g., a subtle red border or background) to make it easily noticeable.

**Revising Widget Display**

I'm currently focused on enhancing the `IntegrationWidget`. My top priority is improving the visual prominence of the info icon, specifically for integrations flagged with `WARN` or `ISSUE` statuses. Additionally, I'm working to give the expanded error messages a clearer visual distinction, perhaps with a slight red border or background.


**Analyzing Error Visibility**

I've been examining the `IntegrationWidget` component more closely. I'm focusing on the error visibility aspect now. My aim is to make the info icon, which appears for `WARN` and `ISSUE` statuses, more noticeable. I'm also planning to use different colors for error messages. Specifically, a red hue for `ISSUE` and amber for `WARN` status, to provide better visual feedback.

entries.description: Refined IntegrationWidget UI: Enhanced info icon visibility and added distinct, color-coded visual styles for expanded error messages based on status (WARN/ISSUE).

payload.text: Add a chart to the HealthWidget to visualize mood, energy, and focus trends over time.

**Visualizing Data Trends**

I'm focusing on the `HealthWidget` component, specifically on enhancing the visualization of mood, energy, and focus data. I've examined the existing "Weekly Pulse" chart, realizing that its manual construction using `div`s is limiting. I think it's time to find something better.


**Refining Chart Implementation**

I'm now integrating `recharts` into the `HealthWidget`. I'm replacing the clunky manual bars with a `LineChart`, aiming for neon-glowing lines to visualize mood, energy, and focus trends. I'm focusing on the visual aspects, ensuring a dark, JACKDASH-consistent theme with subtle grid lines and thin, bright lines. I'm importing `LineChart`, `Line`, and others into the `HealthWidget.tsx` for line chart implementation.


**Integrating Chart Components**

I'm now incorporating `Legend` and `ResponsiveContainer` from `recharts` for the `HealthWidget`. I realized the existing data structure, an array of `HealthEntry` objects, neatly aligns with `recharts`'s dataKey expectations. I also decided `LineChart` is better for tracking trends, so it is the correct choice instead of the `BarChart`. I need to map the `history` data to the chart and make it responsive. I should also theme the tooltip.

entries.description: Added a sophisticated LineChart using Recharts to visualize health trends (mood, energy, focus) over time, replacing the previous manual bar visualization.
